questions.q: The ratio x/y on completion of the above reaction is ______ .
questions.solution: Acid-base reactions are faster than nucleophilic addition reaction. So, in the first step acid-base reaction takes place between alcoholic functional group and Grignard reagent. Now, the second mole of Grignard reagent to react with aldehyde functional group. Hence, at the end of the reaction a diol is produced on acidification.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A sample of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>mg</mi></math> of an unknown monohydric alcohol, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>OH</mi></math> was added to methylmagnesium iodide. A gas is evolved and is collected and its volume measured to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math>. The molecular weight of the unknown alcohol is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>/</mo><mi>mol</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
Alcohols on reaction with Grignard reagent forms hydrocarbons.This is due to the presence of active hydrogen in alcohols and here grignard reagent acts as a base.

When any alcohol is treated with methyl magnemsium iodide the hydrocarbon formed should be methane gas.

ROH+CH3MgI→ROMgI+CH4g

Number of moles of CH4= Number of moles of ROH

⇒Volume(Litres) of the gas22400=mass of the gasMolecular mass of the gas (Assuming NTP Condition)

⇒3.122400=4.5×10-3Molecular mass of the gas

⇒MM=32.51

=33g/mol

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
0.25 g of an organic compound containing chlorine gave 0.40 g of silver chloride in Carius estimation. The percentage of chlorine present in the compound is [in nearest integer]
(Given: Molar mass of Ag is 108 g mol-1 and that of Cl is 35.5 g mol-1)
questions.solution:
Percentage of chlorine in the organic compound=atomic mass of chlorine X mass of AgCl formed X 100molecular mass ofAgCl X weight of organic compound

Mass of organic compound =0.25 g

Mass of AgCl formed =0.40 g

Mass of Cl in organic compound =0.40×35.5143.5×0.25

Percentage of Cl in the compound =0.40×35.5143.5×0.25×100

=39.58%

Nearest integer=40.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of an organic compound was subjected to estimation of nitrogen by Dumas method in which volume of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> evolved (at STP) was found to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>22</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>400</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math>. The percentage of nitrogen in the compound is - [nearest integer] (Given: Molar mass of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>28</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>mol</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>, Molar volume of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>STP</mi><mo>:</mo><mn>22</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></math>)
questions.solution:
Volume of N2 gas is equal to 22.4 ml at STP
Mole of N2 gas = 22.422400=11000
Weight of N2 gas =11000X 28

% of N in organic compound =281000X 1000.2=14%

Hence, The percentage of nitrogen in the compound is 14%

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In bromination of Propyne, with Bromine <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></math>-tetrabromopropane is obtained in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>27</mn><mo>%</mo></math> yield. The amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></math> tetrabromopropane obtained from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of Bromine in this reaction is_____<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> . (Molar Mass : Bromine <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>80</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>/</mo><mi>mol</mi></math>)
questions.solution:
According to the above chemical reaction, 2 moles of bromine will give one mole of the product.

2 moles of Br2 = 2×160 g of Br2 gives one mole of product = 360 g of product

So, mass of product formed from 1 gm of Br2 will be =3602×160 ×1g = 98 g of product

But given that % yield = 27%.

So actual amount of product = 98×27100 = 0.30 gram

=3.0×10-1 g
